source: Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare, India

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi
schemes.description: Direct income support of ₹6,000 per year in three equal installments of ₹2,000 to all landholding farmer families.
enrollment_steps: Go to the official website: pmkisan.gov.in
Click on the 'New Farmer Registration' tab
Select Rural or Urban Farmer Registration and enter your Aadhaar Number and mobile number
Select your state, district, sub-district, block, and village
Fill in personal details, bank details, and land parcel details (survey/survey number, area)
Upload the required documents (Aadhaar Card, Land Deed/Patta)
Submit the application and note down the registration number for tracking status

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana
schemes.description: Subsidized crop insurance covering yield loss due to natural calamities, pests, and diseases.
enrollment_steps: Visit the PMFBY portal: pmfby.gov.in or contact your nearest bank/Common Service Center (CSC)
Click on 'Farmer Corner' and login with your mobile number
Fill in the crop details, land location, state, district, and bank account information
Upload Land Registry document, Tenant agreement (if applicable), and Sowing Certificate
Calculate the premium and pay the farmer's share (2% for Kharif, 1.5% for Rabi, 5% for commercial/horticulture crops) online
Submit the form and download the insurance policy receipt
documents_required: Sowing Certificate (verified by Patwari or Sarpanch)

schemes.name: Kisan Credit Card Scheme
schemes.description: Short-term credit for farmers to meet crop cultivation expenses, post-harvest needs, and domestic consumption at a low interest rate of 4% (with prompt repayment subsidy).
enrollment_steps: Download the KCC application form from pmkisan.gov.in or visit any commercial/cooperative bank
Fill in personal details, landholding size, crops grown, and details of PM-KISAN enrollment
Choose the card limit requested (up to ₹3 Lakhs)
Submit the form along with Aadhaar, Land Records, and Crop Sowing declaration to the bank branch
The bank will verify details and issue the Kisan Credit Card within 14 days
documents_required: Identity Proof (Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, PAN)
No-Dues Certificate from neighboring financial institutions

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Maandhan Yojana
schemes.name_kn: ಪ್ರಧಾನ ಮಂತ್ರಿ ಕಿಸಾನ್ ಮಾನ್ ಧನ್ ಯೋಜನೆ
schemes.description: Voluntary and contributory pension scheme for small and marginal farmers providing a monthly pension of ₹3,000 upon reaching age 60.
enrollment_steps: Go to the nearest Common Service Center (CSC) or visit maandhan.in
Provide Aadhaar card and Bank Account details to the CSC agent or enter them online
Fill the age-specific monthly contribution form (contributions range from ₹55 to ₹200 based on entry age)
Authorize the bank account for auto-debit of monthly contributions
Sign the consent form and receive the Pension Card (Kisan Pension Account Number)

schemes.name: Electronic National Agriculture Market
schemes.description: Pan-India electronic trading portal networking the existing APMC mandis to create a unified national market for agricultural commodities.
enrollment_steps: Visit enam.gov.in and click on the 'Register' link
Select registration type as 'Farmer'
Fill in user profile, state, district, APMC mandi name, and bank account details
Upload a scan of the bank passbook or cancelled cheque
Upon approval by the local mandi administrator, you will receive an e-NAM farmer ID via SMS
Bring your produce to the registered APMC mandi for quality assaying and electronic bidding

schemes.name: Soil Health Card Scheme
schemes.description: Provides soil health cards to farmers every 2-3 years, containing status of 12 soil nutrients and customized fertilizer dosage recommendations.
enrollment_steps: Submit soil samples to the local Agriculture Extension Officer (AEO) or nearest soil testing lab
AEO collects samples with GPS coordinates directly from the farmer's field
Soil samples are tested for 12 parameters (pH, EC, Organic Carbon, N, P, K, S, Zn, Fe, Cu, Mn, B)
The Soil Health Card is generated and distributed directly to the farmer, or it can be downloaded from soilhealth.dac.gov.in using Aadhaar number

schemes.name: Sub-Mission on Agricultural Mechanization
schemes.description: Promotes agricultural mechanization by providing 50% to 80% subsidy for purchasing tractors, power tillers, rotavators, harvesters, and custom hiring centers.
enrollment_steps: Register on the DBT portal: agrimachinery.nic.in
Select your state, district, block, and enter Aadhaar details
Apply for the specific machinery or implement subsidy
Upload land details, category certificate (SC/ST/Women/Small farmer get higher subsidies), and dealer quotation
Await approval of the subsidy application from the district agriculture engineer
Purchase the machine from an authorized dealer, upload the invoice and serial number, and receive DBT subsidy directly in bank account
documents_required: Land Record Book / Record of Rights
Caste Certificate (for SC/ST farmers to claim higher subsidy)
